substitutionGroups.name: Chest Horizontal Press
substitutionGroups.description: Flat and decline chest-pressing movements that train the pecs with shoulders and triceps assisting.

substitutionGroups.name: Chest Incline Press
substitutionGroups.description: Incline and angled pressing variations that bias the upper chest and front delts.

substitutionGroups.name: Chest Fly Isolation
substitutionGroups.description: Chest isolation movements built around a hugging or arcing motion rather than a press.

substitutionGroups.name: Back Vertical Pull
substitutionGroups.description: Lat-dominant vertical pulling movements, including bodyweight and cable options.

substitutionGroups.name: Back Horizontal Row
substitutionGroups.description: Rowing variations for the lats, mid-back, rear delts, and upper-back thickness.

substitutionGroups.name: Lat Isolation
substitutionGroups.description: Straight-arm lat work that trains shoulder extension without heavy elbow flexion.

substitutionGroups.name: Rear Delt & Upper Back
substitutionGroups.description: Rear-delt, external-rotation, and upper-back movements for shoulder balance and posture.

substitutionGroups.name: Quad Squat Pattern
substitutionGroups.description: Squat-pattern lower-body compounds where knee bend and quad drive are the main features.

substitutionGroups.name: Single-Leg Quad & Glute
substitutionGroups.description: Lunge, split-squat, and step-up variations for unilateral leg strength and balance.

substitutionGroups.name: Hip Hinge Pattern
substitutionGroups.description: Hinge movements that load the posterior chain through hip extension.

substitutionGroups.name: Glute Bridge & Thrust
substitutionGroups.description: Hip-extension movements emphasizing glute contraction with a shorter, stable range of motion.

substitutionGroups.name: Quad Isolation
substitutionGroups.description: Knee-extension work that isolates the quadriceps without heavy hip involvement.

substitutionGroups.name: Hamstring Isolation
substitutionGroups.description: Knee-flexion work that isolates the hamstrings.

substitutionGroups.name: Calf Raise
substitutionGroups.description: Ankle-extension work for the calves.

substitutionGroups.name: Shoulder Vertical Press
substitutionGroups.description: Overhead and high-angle pressing variations for the shoulders and triceps.

substitutionGroups.name: Shoulder Raise Isolation
substitutionGroups.description: Front- and side-delt isolation exercises using controlled arm raises.

substitutionGroups.name: Biceps Curl
substitutionGroups.description: Curl variations that train elbow flexion, with some options adding forearm involvement.

substitutionGroups.name: Triceps Extension
substitutionGroups.description: Triceps isolation movements that train elbow extension from cable, dumbbell, or barbell positions.

substitutionGroups.name: Triceps Compound Push
substitutionGroups.description: Compound pushing movements that bias the triceps while still involving chest and shoulders.

substitutionGroups.name: Core Anti-Movement
substitutionGroups.description: Core stability drills that resist extension, rotation, or side-bending rather than chasing motion.

substitutionGroups.name: Core Flexion & Rotation
substitutionGroups.description: Core movements that use controlled trunk flexion, hip flexion, or rotation.

substitutionGroups.name: Loaded Carry & Drive
substitutionGroups.description: Loaded movement patterns that build bracing, grip, conditioning, and total-body output.

substitutionGroups.name: Hip Abduction Isolation
substitutionGroups.description: Outer-hip isolation work that trains moving the legs away from the midline.

substitutionGroups.name: Hip Adduction Isolation
substitutionGroups.description: Inner-thigh isolation work that trains bringing the legs toward the midline.

substitutionGroups.name: Forearm Wrist Isolation
substitutionGroups.description: Wrist-flexion and wrist-extension movements for direct forearm work.

substitutionGroups.name: Trap Shrug
substitutionGroups.description: Shrug variations that train scapular elevation and upper-trap strength.

substitutionGroups.name: Conditioning & Cardio
substitutionGroups.description: Conditioning movements and machines used for intervals, circuits, and metabolic finishers.

bench_press.description: A foundational barbell chest press for building pressing strength through the chest, shoulders, and triceps.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with your eyes under the bar and your feet planted firmly on the floor.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together, keep your upper back tight, and grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width.
Unrack the bar over your mid-chest, brace, and lower it under control toward the lower-to-mid chest.
Press the bar up and slightly back until your elbows are locked without bouncing or losing your shoulder position.
formCues: Keep shoulder blades set against the bench or floor.
Press with wrists stacked over elbows.
Control the lowering phase before driving up.
commonMistakes: Flaring elbows too wide.
Bouncing out of the bottom.
Losing shoulder position or letting hips lift.

incline_dumbbell_press.description: An upper-chest dumbbell press that allows each arm to move independently through a deep, controlled range.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a low-to-moderate incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand.
Lie back, plant your feet, and start with the dumbbells beside your upper chest.
Press the dumbbells upward while keeping your wrists stacked over your elbows.
Lower with control until you feel a chest stretch, then repeat without letting the shoulders shrug forward.
formCues: Use a low-to-moderate incline rather than turning it into a shoulder press.
Keep ribs down and shoulder blades stable.
Press up and slightly back in a natural arc.
commonMistakes: Setting the bench too steep.
Overarching the lower back.
Letting the weights drift too far forward.

push_up.description: A bodyweight horizontal press that trains the chest, shoulders, triceps, and core as one unit.
instructions: Start in a high plank with hands just outside shoulder width and feet set behind you.
Brace your abs and glutes so your body forms one straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the floor with elbows angled slightly back rather than flared straight out.
Press the floor away until your elbows are straight, keeping your hips and ribs from sagging.
formCues: Keep shoulder blades set against the bench or floor.
Press with wrists stacked over elbows.
Control the lowering phase before driving up.
commonMistakes: Flaring elbows too wide.
Bouncing out of the bottom.
Losing shoulder position or letting hips lift.

dumbbell_press.description: A flat dumbbell chest press that builds pressing strength while allowing a natural arm path.
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell on each thigh, then lie back and bring the weights to chest level.
Plant your feet and pull your shoulder blades gently back and down.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are straight without clanking the weights together.
Lower until the dumbbells reach chest height or a comfortable stretch, then repeat under control.
formCues: Keep shoulder blades set against the bench or floor.
Press with wrists stacked over elbows.
Control the lowering phase before driving up.
commonMistakes: Flaring elbows too wide.
Bouncing out of the bottom.
Losing shoulder position or letting hips lift.

cable_fly.description: A cable chest isolation movement that keeps tension on the pecs through the entire fly arc.
instructions: Set the cable pulleys around chest height and take one handle in each hand.
Step forward into a stable stance with a slight bend in the elbows and chest lifted.
Bring your hands together in a wide hugging motion, squeezing the chest at the finish.
Let the handles open back out under control without letting the shoulders roll forward.
formCues: Keep a soft, fixed bend in the elbows.
Move in a wide hugging arc.
Stop the stretch before the shoulders roll forward.
commonMistakes: Going too heavy and turning it into a press.
Dropping too deep into shoulder discomfort.
Changing elbow bend during the rep.

dumbbell_fly.description: A dumbbell chest fly that emphasizes a deep pec stretch and controlled adduction rather than heavy pressing.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with dumbbells held above your chest and palms facing each other.
Keep a soft bend in the elbows and set your shoulder blades against the bench.
Open your arms in a wide arc until you feel a comfortable chest stretch.
Squeeze the chest to bring the dumbbells back over the chest without turning it into a press.
formCues: Keep a soft, fixed bend in the elbows.
Move in a wide hugging arc.
Stop the stretch before the shoulders roll forward.
commonMistakes: Going too heavy and turning it into a press.
Dropping too deep into shoulder discomfort.
Changing elbow bend during the rep.

machine_chest_press.description: A guided chest press that gives a stable pressing path and is easy to load progressively.
instructions: Adjust the seat so the handles line up with your mid-chest.
Sit tall with your back against the pad and feet planted.
Press the handles forward until your arms are nearly straight without shrugging.
Return slowly until your elbows come slightly behind the body or the machine reaches its safe stop.
formCues: Keep shoulder blades set against the bench or floor.
Press with wrists stacked over elbows.
Control the lowering phase before driving up.
commonMistakes: Flaring elbows too wide.
Bouncing out of the bottom.
Losing shoulder position or letting hips lift.

decline_bench_press.description: A decline barbell press that shifts emphasis toward the lower chest while still training heavy pressing strength.
instructions: Secure your legs on the decline bench and lie back with your eyes under the bar.
Grip slightly wider than shoulder width and pull the shoulder blades back into the bench.
Unrack the bar and lower it with control toward the lower chest.
Press back to the start without bouncing or letting the shoulders slide forward.
formCues: Keep shoulder blades set against the bench or floor.
Press with wrists stacked over elbows.
Control the lowering phase before driving up.
commonMistakes: Flaring elbows too wide.
Bouncing out of the bottom.
Losing shoulder position or letting hips lift.

pull_up.description: A bodyweight vertical pull for the lats and upper back using an overhand grip.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Brace your abs and start the pull by driving your elbows down toward your ribs.
Pull until your chin clears the bar or your upper chest approaches it.
Lower with control to a full hang, keeping the rep smooth instead of swinging.
formCues: Initiate by driving elbows down, not by shrugging.
Keep the chest proud and ribs controlled.
Lower to a full controlled stretch.
commonMistakes: Pulling with the biceps only.
Swinging or using body English.
Stopping short of a controlled stretch.

lat_pulldown.description: A cable vertical pull that trains the lats with adjustable resistance and a stable seated setup.
instructions: Adjust the thigh pad so your legs are secure and grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width.
Sit tall, lean back slightly, and keep your ribs down.
Pull the bar toward your upper chest by driving elbows down and back.
Return the bar upward with control until the arms are long and the lats stretch.
formCues: Initiate by driving elbows down, not by shrugging.
Keep the chest proud and ribs controlled.
Lower to a full controlled stretch.
commonMistakes: Pulling with the biceps only.
Swinging or using body English.
Stopping short of a controlled stretch.

barbell_row.description: A loaded horizontal pull for building back thickness, hip-hinge strength, and upper-back control.
instructions: Stand with the barbell in your hands and hinge at the hips until your torso is angled forward.
Brace your core, keep the spine neutral, and let the bar hang below your shoulders.
Row the bar toward your lower ribs by pulling elbows back.
Lower under control without standing up or bouncing the bar.
formCues: Row elbows toward the hips or lower ribs.
Keep the torso stable throughout the rep.
Squeeze the back without yanking with the arms.
commonMistakes: Jerking the weight with momentum.
Rounding the back or craning the neck.
Shrugging instead of rowing.

seated_cable_row.description: A stable cable row that trains the lats and mid-back through a smooth horizontal pull.
instructions: Sit at the cable row with feet supported and knees slightly bent.
Hold the handle with arms extended and torso tall, then brace your trunk.
Pull the handle toward your lower ribs while squeezing the shoulder blades back.
Return slowly until the arms are long without collapsing your posture.
formCues: Row elbows toward the hips or lower ribs.
Keep the torso stable throughout the rep.
Squeeze the back without yanking with the arms.
commonMistakes: Jerking the weight with momentum.
Rounding the back or craning the neck.
Shrugging instead of rowing.

dumbbell_row.description: A single-arm dumbbell row that targets the lats and mid-back with support from a bench.
instructions: Place one hand and knee on a bench, with the opposite foot planted and a dumbbell hanging below the shoulder.
Keep your torso stable and your spine long.
Row the dumbbell toward your hip by driving the elbow back.
Lower until the arm is long and the lat stretches, then repeat without twisting your torso.
formCues: Row elbows toward the hips or lower ribs.
Keep the torso stable throughout the rep.
Squeeze the back without yanking with the arms.
commonMistakes: Jerking the weight with momentum.
Rounding the back or craning the neck.
Shrugging instead of rowing.

t_bar_row.description: A heavy row variation using a T-bar or landmine setup to train the lats, mid-back, and traps.
instructions: Set up on the T-bar row platform or straddle a landmine bar with a suitable handle.
Hinge at the hips, brace, and keep your chest proud.
Row the handle toward your lower chest or upper abdomen.
Lower with control until the arms are extended without rounding the back.
formCues: Row elbows toward the hips or lower ribs.
Keep the torso stable throughout the rep.
Squeeze the back without yanking with the arms.
commonMistakes: Jerking the weight with momentum.
Rounding the back or craning the neck.
Shrugging instead of rowing.

face_pull.description: A cable upper-back and rear-delt movement that reinforces shoulder external rotation and posture.
instructions: Attach a rope to a cable at upper-chest or face height and hold one end in each hand.
Step back, set your ribs down, and start with arms extended.
Pull the rope toward your face while separating the hands and rotating the thumbs back.
Pause briefly, then return under control without letting the shoulders shrug.
formCues: Lead with elbows and rear delts.
Keep shoulders down away from the ears.
Use light enough weight to pause at the contracted position.
commonMistakes: Using traps instead of rear delts.
Going too heavy and losing the path.
Arching the back to finish reps.

chest_supported_row.description: A supported row that removes most lower-back demand so the upper back can be trained hard and cleanly.
instructions: Set an incline bench or machine so your chest is supported throughout the row.
Let the weights hang under your shoulders with your neck neutral.
Row by pulling elbows back and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Lower slowly until the arms are long without lifting your chest off the pad.
formCues: Row elbows toward the hips or lower ribs.
Keep the torso stable throughout the rep.
Squeeze the back without yanking with the arms.
commonMistakes: Jerking the weight with momentum.
Rounding the back or craning the neck.
Shrugging instead of rowing.

squat.description: A barbell back squat for building lower-body strength through the quads, glutes, and trunk.
instructions: Set the bar securely on your upper back and step out with feet about shoulder width apart.
Brace your core, keep the chest proud, and unlock hips and knees together.
Squat down with knees tracking over toes until you reach your controlled depth.
Drive through the mid-foot to stand tall without letting the knees cave in.
formCues: Brace before each rep.
Track knees in line with toes.
Keep pressure through the mid-foot.
commonMistakes: Knees collapsing inward.
Heels lifting off the floor or platform.
Losing brace at the bottom.

front_squat.description: A front-loaded squat that emphasizes the quads, upright torso strength, and core bracing.
instructions: Set the bar in the front rack across the shoulders with elbows high.
Stand with feet around shoulder width and brace hard through the trunk.
Descend by bending knees and hips while keeping the torso upright.
Drive up through the mid-foot, keeping elbows lifted so the bar does not roll forward.
formCues: Brace before each rep.
Track knees in line with toes.
Keep pressure through the mid-foot.
commonMistakes: Knees collapsing inward.
Heels lifting off the floor or platform.
Losing brace at the bottom.

leg_press.description: A machine squat-pattern movement that loads the quads and glutes with a supported torso.
instructions: Sit in the leg press with your back and hips firmly against the pad.
Place your feet on the platform around shoulder width and unlock the safety handles.
Lower the platform until your knees bend deeply while your hips stay on the pad.
Press through the mid-foot to extend the legs without locking the knees aggressively.
formCues: Brace before each rep.
Track knees in line with toes.
Keep pressure through the mid-foot.
commonMistakes: Knees collapsing inward.
Heels lifting off the floor or platform.
Losing brace at the bottom.

romanian_deadlift.description: A hip-hinge movement that emphasizes hamstring loading, glute tension, and controlled eccentric strength.
instructions: Stand tall holding the barbell in front of your thighs with a slight bend in the knees.
Brace and push your hips back while keeping the bar close to your legs.
Lower until you feel a strong hamstring stretch without rounding your back.
Drive the hips forward to stand tall, squeezing the glutes at the top.
formCues: Push hips back before bending more at the knees.
Keep the load close to your body.
Stand by driving hips through, not by leaning back.
commonMistakes: Rounding the lower back.
Letting the load drift away from the body.
Squatting the movement instead of hinging.

deadlift.description: A heavy floor pull that trains the posterior chain, trunk, grip, and total-body strength.
instructions: Stand with the bar over your mid-foot and your feet about hip width apart.
Hinge down, grip the bar, and pull your chest up until the back is tight and the arms are straight.
Push the floor away and stand up with the bar staying close to your legs.
Return the bar to the floor with control by hinging the hips back and bending the knees as needed.
formCues: Take the slack out of the bar before it leaves the floor.
Push the floor away rather than yanking with the back.
Keep the bar close from floor to lockout.
commonMistakes: Yanking the bar before getting tight.
Letting hips shoot up before the chest.
Finishing by leaning back excessively.

hip_thrust.description: A loaded glute movement that trains powerful hip extension with the upper back supported on a bench.
instructions: Sit with your upper back against a bench and the barbell padded across your hips.
Set your feet around hip width so your shins are near vertical at the top.
Brace and drive through your heels to lift the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor.
Squeeze the glutes at the top, then lower under control without overextending the lower back.
formCues: Finish with glutes, not lower back.
Keep ribs down at the top.
Use a strong heel or mid-foot drive.
commonMistakes: Overarching the lower back at the top.
Feet placed too far away to use the glutes well.
Bouncing reps without a glute squeeze.

walking_lunge.description: A dynamic single-leg movement that trains quads, glutes, balance, and coordination.
instructions: Stand tall with bodyweight or dumbbells at your sides.
Step forward and lower until both knees bend, keeping the front knee tracking over the toes.
Drive through the front foot to stand and bring the back leg forward into the next rep.
Continue alternating legs while keeping your torso controlled and upright.
formCues: Let the front leg do the work.
Keep hips square and torso controlled.
Drive through the whole foot, not just the toes.
commonMistakes: Pushing mainly off the back leg.
Letting the front knee cave inward.
Rushing the descent and losing balance.

bulgarian_split_squat.description: A rear-foot-elevated split squat that heavily loads the front-leg quads and glutes.
instructions: Stand a stride in front of a bench and place the top of your rear foot on it.
Square your hips and keep most of your weight on the front foot.
Lower under control until the front thigh approaches parallel or your comfortable depth.
Drive through the front foot to return to standing without pushing off the rear leg.
formCues: Let the front leg do the work.
Keep hips square and torso controlled.
Drive through the whole foot, not just the toes.
commonMistakes: Pushing mainly off the back leg.
Letting the front knee cave inward.
Rushing the descent and losing balance.

leg_extension.description: A machine knee-extension movement that directly targets the quadriceps.
instructions: Adjust the machine so your knees line up with the pivot and the pad rests above your ankles.
Sit back firmly and hold the handles for stability.
Extend your knees until the legs are straight and the quads are squeezed.
Lower slowly back to the starting position without letting the stack slam.
formCues: Line your knees up with the machine pivot.
Squeeze the quads at the top.
Control the weight on the way down.
commonMistakes: Lifting hips off the seat.
Swinging the weight up.
Letting the stack slam between reps.

leg_curl.description: A machine hamstring isolation exercise that trains knee flexion through a controlled range.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the pivot point.
Set the pad just above your heels or ankles and keep your hips anchored.
Curl the pad toward your body by bending the knees and squeezing the hamstrings.
Return slowly until the legs are extended without losing tension.
formCues: Keep hips anchored to the pad.
Curl smoothly without jerking.
Control the return to full length.
commonMistakes: Hips lifting off the pad.
Using momentum to curl.
Cutting the eccentric short.

calf_raise.description: A standing calf movement that trains ankle extension and lower-leg strength.
instructions: Stand with the balls of your feet on the platform and heels free to move.
Keep your knees mostly straight and hold the support handles if available.
Rise onto the balls of your feet and squeeze the calves at the top.
Lower the heels under control until you feel a calf stretch, then repeat.
formCues: Pause briefly at the top.
Lower into a full controlled stretch.
Keep pressure through the big toe and second toe.
commonMistakes: Bouncing through the Achilles tendon.
Skipping the bottom stretch.
Letting ankles roll outward.

dumbbell_shoulder_press.description: A dumbbell overhead press for shoulder strength with each arm moving independently.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell at each shoulder with palms facing forward or slightly inward.
Brace your abs and keep ribs stacked over hips.
Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are straight and biceps are near your ears.
Lower back to shoulder height under control without arching your lower back.
formCues: Brace ribs down before pressing.
Finish with biceps near ears.
Press without shrugging into your neck.
commonMistakes: Leaning back excessively.
Pressing with wrists bent back.
Shrugging before the arms finish.

overhead_press.description: A strict barbell overhead press that builds shoulders, triceps, and standing trunk strength.
instructions: Set the bar at upper-chest height and grip just outside shoulder width.
Unrack with the bar on the front of the shoulders, brace, and squeeze the glutes.
Press the bar upward, moving your head slightly back so the bar can travel straight.
Lock out overhead, then lower the bar to the shoulders under control.
formCues: Brace ribs down before pressing.
Finish with biceps near ears.
Press without shrugging into your neck.
commonMistakes: Leaning back excessively.
Pressing with wrists bent back.
Shrugging before the arms finish.

lateral_raise.description: A side-delt isolation movement that builds shoulder width with light, controlled raises.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace and raise the arms out to the sides until they reach about shoulder height.
Lead with the elbows and keep the shoulders down away from the ears.
Lower slowly without swinging the weights into the next rep.
formCues: Lead with the elbows.
Use a controlled tempo.
Stop around shoulder height unless mobility allows more.
commonMistakes: Swinging the weights.
Shrugging up into the neck.
Using weights too heavy for control.

front_raise.description: A front-delt isolation movement using controlled shoulder flexion.
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells in front of your thighs.
Brace your core and keep a soft bend in the elbows.
Raise the weights in front of you to about shoulder height.
Lower slowly without leaning back or swinging.
formCues: Lead with the elbows.
Use a controlled tempo.
Stop around shoulder height unless mobility allows more.
commonMistakes: Swinging the weights.
Shrugging up into the neck.
Using weights too heavy for control.

rear_delt_fly.description: A rear-delt isolation movement that trains shoulder extension and upper-back control.
instructions: Hinge forward with dumbbells hanging below your shoulders or set up on cables.
Keep a soft bend in the elbows and your neck neutral.
Raise the arms out to the sides until the rear delts and upper back contract.
Lower slowly without turning the movement into a row.
formCues: Lead with elbows and rear delts.
Keep shoulders down away from the ears.
Use light enough weight to pause at the contracted position.
commonMistakes: Using traps instead of rear delts.
Going too heavy and losing the path.
Arching the back to finish reps.

arnold_press.description: A rotating dumbbell shoulder press that combines front-delt loading with overhead pressing.
instructions: Start seated or standing with dumbbells in front of your shoulders, palms facing you.
Brace your trunk and begin pressing while rotating the palms forward.
Finish overhead with arms straight and shoulders controlled.
Reverse the motion slowly back to the front-rack start position.
formCues: Brace ribs down before pressing.
Finish with biceps near ears.
Press without shrugging into your neck.
commonMistakes: Leaning back excessively.
Pressing with wrists bent back.
Shrugging before the arms finish.

barbell_curl.description: A classic two-arm curl for loading the biceps with a stable bar path.
instructions: Stand tall holding a barbell with an underhand grip around shoulder width.
Keep elbows close to your sides and brace your torso.
Curl the bar upward by bending the elbows until the biceps are fully contracted.
Lower slowly to straight arms without swinging your hips.
formCues: Keep elbows close to the torso.
Use a full squeeze at the top.
Lower slowly to straight arms.
commonMistakes: Swinging the torso.
Letting elbows drift far forward.
Only performing the top half of the rep.

dumbbell_curl.description: A dumbbell biceps curl that allows each arm to work independently with a natural wrist position.
instructions: Stand or sit with a dumbbell in each hand and arms hanging at your sides.
Keep elbows close to the ribs and shoulders relaxed.
Curl the dumbbells up while turning the palms upward if using a supinated curl.
Lower with control until the arms are straight.
formCues: Keep elbows close to the torso.
Use a full squeeze at the top.
Lower slowly to straight arms.
commonMistakes: Swinging the torso.
Letting elbows drift far forward.
Only performing the top half of the rep.

hammer_curl.description: A neutral-grip curl that trains the biceps, brachialis, and forearms.
instructions: Hold dumbbells at your sides with palms facing each other.
Keep the upper arms still and elbows close to your torso.
Curl the dumbbells upward while maintaining the neutral grip.
Lower slowly without letting the shoulders roll forward.
formCues: Keep elbows close to the torso.
Use a full squeeze at the top.
Lower slowly to straight arms.
commonMistakes: Swinging the torso.
Letting elbows drift far forward.
Only performing the top half of the rep.

preacher_curl.description: A supported curl that locks the upper arms in place and makes the biceps work through a strict range.
instructions: Set your upper arms on the preacher pad with the elbows supported.
Start with the arm nearly straight while keeping the shoulder settled.
Curl the weight upward until the biceps contract hard.
Lower slowly without relaxing at the bottom or lifting the upper arm off the pad.
formCues: Keep elbows close to the torso.
Use a full squeeze at the top.
Lower slowly to straight arms.
commonMistakes: Swinging the torso.
Letting elbows drift far forward.
Only performing the top half of the rep.

triceps_pushdown.description: A cable triceps isolation movement that trains elbow extension with steady tension.
instructions: Attach a bar or handle to a high cable and stand close to the stack.
Pin your elbows by your sides and set your shoulders down.
Push the handle down until the elbows are fully extended and the triceps squeeze.
Return under control until the forearms rise without the elbows drifting forward.
formCues: Keep upper arms as still as possible.
Reach full elbow extension at the finish.
Control the stretch without letting shoulders take over.
commonMistakes: Letting elbows flare or drift.
Turning the movement into a press.
Rushing the stretched position.

skull_crusher.description: A lying triceps extension that loads elbow extension with the upper arms held mostly still.
instructions: Lie on a bench holding a bar above your chest with arms straight.
Angle your upper arms slightly back and keep them fixed.
Bend the elbows to lower the bar toward the forehead or just behind the head.
Extend the elbows to return to the start without flaring the upper arms.
formCues: Keep upper arms as still as possible.
Reach full elbow extension at the finish.
Control the stretch without letting shoulders take over.
commonMistakes: Letting elbows flare or drift.
Turning the movement into a press.
Rushing the stretched position.

overhead_triceps_extension.description: An overhead triceps movement that emphasizes the long head of the triceps in a stretched position.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell overhead with both hands or one hand depending on the variation.
Keep your ribs down and elbows pointing mostly forward.
Lower the weight behind your head by bending the elbows.
Extend the elbows to raise the weight back overhead without arching your back.
formCues: Keep upper arms as still as possible.
Reach full elbow extension at the finish.
Control the stretch without letting shoulders take over.
commonMistakes: Letting elbows flare or drift.
Turning the movement into a press.
Rushing the stretched position.

dips.description: A bodyweight compound press that can bias the triceps or chest depending on torso angle.
instructions: Support yourself on parallel bars or a bench with arms straight and shoulders controlled.
Brace and lower by bending the elbows, keeping the shoulders from shrugging up.
Descend only as far as your shoulders tolerate comfortably.
Press back up to straight arms without bouncing at the bottom.
formCues: Keep elbows tracking close to the body.
Use a controlled bottom position.
Press to lockout without losing shoulder position.
commonMistakes: Flaring elbows too wide.
Dropping into shoulder discomfort.
Using partial reps without control.

plank.description: A core stability hold that trains the abs to resist spinal extension.
instructions: Set your forearms on the floor with elbows under shoulders and legs extended behind you.
Brace your abs, squeeze your glutes, and create a straight line from head to heels.
Hold the position while breathing slowly and keeping the ribs pulled down.
Stop the set when your hips sag, your lower back arches, or you lose tension.
formCues: Brace as if resisting movement.
Keep ribs stacked over pelvis.
Breathe without losing tension.
commonMistakes: Holding breath the entire time.
Letting ribs flare or hips sag.
Choosing a version too advanced to control.

hanging_leg_raise.description: A hanging core exercise that trains the abs and hip flexors while challenging grip and body control.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with shoulders active and legs together.
Brace your abs and slightly tuck the pelvis before lifting the legs.
Raise knees or straight legs toward the chest without swinging.
Lower under control to a still hang before starting the next rep.
formCues: Move through the trunk, not just the arms.
Keep the motion controlled.
Stop before the lower back takes over.
commonMistakes: Using momentum instead of abdominal control.
Pulling on the neck or shoulders.
Arching the lower back between reps.

cable_crunch.description: A weighted kneeling crunch that loads spinal flexion through the abs.
instructions: Attach a rope to a high cable and kneel facing the stack.
Hold the rope near your head and set your hips back slightly.
Crunch by bringing ribs toward pelvis while keeping the hips mostly fixed.
Return slowly until the abs stretch without letting the weight pull you upright.
formCues: Move through the trunk, not just the arms.
Keep the motion controlled.
Stop before the lower back takes over.
commonMistakes: Using momentum instead of abdominal control.
Pulling on the neck or shoulders.
Arching the lower back between reps.

russian_twist.description: A rotational core movement for the obliques using bodyweight or a light load.
instructions: Sit on the floor with knees bent and torso leaned back slightly.
Brace your abs and hold your hands or a light weight in front of your chest.
Rotate your ribcage side to side with control rather than just swinging your arms.
Keep the movement smooth and stop before your lower back takes over.
formCues: Move through the trunk, not just the arms.
Keep the motion controlled.
Stop before the lower back takes over.
commonMistakes: Using momentum instead of abdominal control.
Pulling on the neck or shoulders.
Arching the lower back between reps.

pallof_press.description: An anti-rotation core exercise that teaches the trunk to resist a sideways cable or band pull.
instructions: Set a cable or band at chest height and stand sideways to the anchor.
Hold the handle at your chest and step away until you feel sideways tension.
Brace and press the handle straight out in front of you without rotating.
Pause, then bring it back to your chest under control before repeating.
formCues: Brace as if resisting movement.
Keep ribs stacked over pelvis.
Breathe without losing tension.
commonMistakes: Holding breath the entire time.
Letting ribs flare or hips sag.
Choosing a version too advanced to control.

farmers_carry.description: A loaded carry that builds grip, traps, posture, bracing, and work capacity.
instructions: Pick up a heavy dumbbell or kettlebell in each hand using a strong hinge position.
Stand tall with shoulders down and ribs stacked over hips.
Walk for the target distance or time using short, controlled steps.
Set the weights down with a hinge rather than rounding your back.
formCues: Stay tall and braced.
Use short, powerful steps.
Keep shoulders controlled and stable.
commonMistakes: Leaning back or twisting under load.
Taking sloppy, oversized steps.
Dropping posture as fatigue builds.

kettlebell_swing.description: A ballistic hip-hinge movement that trains glute and hamstring power with conditioning carryover.
instructions: Place the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle with both hands.
Hike the bell back between your legs while keeping your spine neutral.
Snap the hips forward to swing the bell to chest height without lifting with the arms.
Let the bell float down and guide it back into the next hinge.
formCues: Snap the hips; do not lift with the shoulders.
Let the bell float at the top.
Keep the hinge crisp and athletic.
commonMistakes: Squatting the swing instead of hinging.
Lifting the bell with the arms.
Letting the lower back overextend at the top.

goblet_squat.description: A beginner-friendly front-loaded squat that reinforces depth, posture, and bracing.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell or dumbbell close to your chest with elbows pointed down.
Stand with feet around shoulder width and toes slightly out.
Squat down by bending knees and hips while keeping the weight close to your body.
Drive through the mid-foot to stand tall without letting the knees collapse inward.
formCues: Brace before each rep.
Track knees in line with toes.
Keep pressure through the mid-foot.
commonMistakes: Knees collapsing inward.
Heels lifting off the floor or platform.
Losing brace at the bottom.

trap_bar_deadlift.description: A deadlift variation using a trap bar that blends hinge and squat mechanics with a more centered load.
instructions: Step inside the trap bar with feet around hip width and handles centered at your sides.
Hinge and bend the knees to grip the handles, then brace and set your back.
Drive through the floor to stand tall with the handles staying close to your sides.
Lower under control by sending hips back and bending the knees.
formCues: Push hips back before bending more at the knees.
Keep the load close to your body.
Stand by driving hips through, not by leaning back.
commonMistakes: Rounding the lower back.
Letting the load drift away from the body.
Squatting the movement instead of hinging.

sumo_deadlift.description: A wide-stance deadlift that emphasizes glutes, adductors, and a more upright pulling position.
instructions: Stand with a wide stance and toes turned out, with the bar over your mid-foot.
Grip the bar inside your knees and pull your chest up to set your back.
Push the floor apart and stand by extending hips and knees together.
Return the bar to the floor under control while keeping it close.
formCues: Push hips back before bending more at the knees.
Keep the load close to your body.
Stand by driving hips through, not by leaning back.
commonMistakes: Rounding the lower back.
Letting the load drift away from the body.
Squatting the movement instead of hinging.

good_morning.description: A barbell hinge that loads the hamstrings and lower back with the bar on the upper back.
instructions: Set a barbell on your upper back as you would for a back squat.
Stand with knees slightly bent and brace your trunk hard.
Hinge at the hips, sending them back while keeping your spine neutral.
Stand back up by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes.
formCues: Push hips back before bending more at the knees.
Keep the load close to your body.
Stand by driving hips through, not by leaning back.
commonMistakes: Rounding the lower back.
Letting the load drift away from the body.
Squatting the movement instead of hinging.

hip_abduction.description: A machine isolation movement for the outer hips, glute medius, and abductors.
instructions: Sit in the machine with your knees or thighs against the pads.
Set your pelvis and back firmly against the seat.
Drive the legs outward against the pads and squeeze the outer hips.
Return slowly toward the start without letting the weight stack slam.
formCues: Keep pelvis planted against the seat.
Squeeze the outer hips at the end range.
Return slowly without bouncing.
commonMistakes: Rocking the torso to move the weight.
Using a range that causes hip pinching.
Letting the stack slam.

hip_adduction.description: A machine isolation movement for the inner thighs and adductors.
instructions: Sit in the machine with the pads against the inside of your knees or thighs.
Keep your back against the seat and brace lightly through the trunk.
Bring your legs together by squeezing the inner thighs.
Return slowly to the open position without bouncing.
formCues: Keep pelvis planted against the seat.
Squeeze the inner thighs at the end range.
Return slowly without bouncing.
commonMistakes: Bouncing out of the stretched position.
Rocking the torso to move the weight.
Letting the stack slam.

glute_bridge.description: A bodyweight hip-extension movement that teaches glute contraction and pelvic control.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor about hip width apart.
Brace your abs lightly and tuck the pelvis so your lower back is not overarched.
Drive through your heels to lift your hips until knees, hips, and shoulders form a line.
Squeeze the glutes at the top, then lower under control.
formCues: Finish with glutes, not lower back.
Keep ribs down at the top.
Use a strong heel or mid-foot drive.
commonMistakes: Overarching the lower back at the top.
Feet placed too far away to use the glutes well.
Bouncing reps without a glute squeeze.

step_up.description: A unilateral leg exercise that trains the quads and glutes while reinforcing balance and hip control.
instructions: Stand facing a stable box or bench with one foot fully planted on top.
Lean slightly forward and drive through the working foot to stand up on the box.
Bring the other foot up only after the working leg has done the lift.
Step down with control and repeat on the same side or alternate sides.
formCues: Let the front leg do the work.
Keep hips square and torso controlled.
Drive through the whole foot, not just the toes.
commonMistakes: Pushing mainly off the back leg.
Letting the front knee cave inward.
Rushing the descent and losing balance.

box_squat.description: A squat variation that uses a box or bench to control depth and reinforce sitting back with tension.
instructions: Set a box or bench at your target depth and position it behind you in the squat rack.
Unrack the bar on your upper back and brace before descending.
Sit back to the box with control, touching lightly while staying tight.
Drive through the feet to stand without rocking or relaxing on the box.
formCues: Brace before each rep.
Track knees in line with toes.
Keep pressure through the mid-foot.
commonMistakes: Knees collapsing inward.
Heels lifting off the floor or platform.
Losing brace at the bottom.

pendlay_row.description: A strict barbell row from the floor that builds explosive upper-back pulling strength.
instructions: Set the bar on the floor and hinge until your torso is close to parallel with the ground.
Brace your trunk and grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width.
Row the bar explosively toward your lower chest while keeping the torso fixed.
Return the bar to a dead stop on the floor before the next rep.
formCues: Row elbows toward the hips or lower ribs.
Keep the torso stable throughout the rep.
Squeeze the back without yanking with the arms.
commonMistakes: Jerking the weight with momentum.
Rounding the back or craning the neck.
Shrugging instead of rowing.

chin_up.description: A supinated-grip vertical pull that trains the lats with extra biceps involvement.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with palms facing you and hands around shoulder width.
Brace your core and pull your elbows down toward your ribs.
Pull until your chin clears the bar or your chest approaches it.
Lower with control to a full hang without swinging.
formCues: Initiate by driving elbows down, not by shrugging.
Keep the chest proud and ribs controlled.
Lower to a full controlled stretch.
commonMistakes: Pulling with the biceps only.
Swinging or using body English.
Stopping short of a controlled stretch.

straight_arm_pulldown.description: A cable lat isolation exercise that trains shoulder extension with minimal biceps involvement.
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high cable and stand facing the stack.
Hinge slightly forward with arms long and elbows softly bent.
Pull the handle down toward your thighs by driving the upper arms back.
Return slowly overhead until the lats stretch without shrugging.
formCues: Keep arms long with only a soft elbow bend.
Drive the upper arms down toward your sides.
Feel the lats stretch on the way up.
commonMistakes: Bending the elbows into a triceps pushdown.
Shrugging at the top.
Using too much torso swing.

inverted_row.description: A bodyweight row that scales easily by changing body angle and foot position.
instructions: Set a bar at about waist height and lie underneath it with hands just outside shoulder width.
Keep your body straight from shoulders to heels and brace your glutes.
Pull your chest toward the bar by driving elbows back.
Lower until your arms are straight without letting your hips sag.
formCues: Row elbows toward the hips or lower ribs.
Keep the torso stable throughout the rep.
Squeeze the back without yanking with the arms.
commonMistakes: Jerking the weight with momentum.
Rounding the back or craning the neck.
Shrugging instead of rowing.

landmine_press.description: An angled pressing movement that trains the shoulders and upper chest with a shoulder-friendly arc.
instructions: Anchor one end of a barbell in a landmine or secure corner and hold the other end at shoulder height.
Stand tall or half-kneel with ribs down and core braced.
Press the bar up and forward along its natural arc until the arm is extended.
Lower back to the shoulder under control without twisting.
formCues: Use a low-to-moderate incline rather than turning it into a shoulder press.
Keep ribs down and shoulder blades stable.
Press up and slightly back in a natural arc.
commonMistakes: Setting the bench too steep.
Overarching the lower back.
Letting the weights drift too far forward.

push_press.description: A power-focused overhead press that uses leg drive to move heavier loads overhead.
instructions: Start with the bar on the front of your shoulders and hands just outside shoulder width.
Dip a few inches by bending knees while keeping the torso vertical.
Drive hard through the legs and transfer that force into pressing the bar overhead.
Lock out overhead, then lower the bar back to the shoulders under control.
formCues: Brace ribs down before pressing.
Finish with biceps near ears.
Press without shrugging into your neck.
commonMistakes: Leaning back excessively.
Pressing with wrists bent back.
Shrugging before the arms finish.

close_grip_bench.description: A bench press variation with a closer grip that shifts more work to the triceps.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench and grip the bar around shoulder width or slightly narrower.
Set your shoulder blades, plant your feet, and unrack the bar over your chest.
Lower the bar toward the lower chest with elbows tucked closer to your sides.
Press back up by driving through the triceps without letting the wrists bend back.
formCues: Keep elbows tracking close to the body.
Use a controlled bottom position.
Press to lockout without losing shoulder position.
commonMistakes: Flaring elbows too wide.
Dropping into shoulder discomfort.
Using partial reps without control.

cable_crossover.description: A cable chest isolation exercise where the hands cross slightly to maximize pec contraction.
instructions: Set the cable pulleys high and take a handle in each hand.
Step forward into a split stance with chest lifted and elbows softly bent.
Sweep the handles down and across the body in a controlled arc.
Return slowly to the stretched position without letting the shoulders roll forward.
formCues: Keep a soft, fixed bend in the elbows.
Move in a wide hugging arc.
Stop the stretch before the shoulders roll forward.
commonMistakes: Going too heavy and turning it into a press.
Dropping too deep into shoulder discomfort.
Changing elbow bend during the rep.

pec_deck.description: A machine chest fly that isolates the pecs with a fixed path and stable seated position.
instructions: Adjust the seat so the handles or pads line up with your chest.
Sit back with your shoulder blades gently set and elbows slightly bent.
Bring the handles or pads together by squeezing the chest.
Return slowly until you feel a comfortable chest stretch.
formCues: Keep a soft, fixed bend in the elbows.
Move in a wide hugging arc.
Stop the stretch before the shoulders roll forward.
commonMistakes: Going too heavy and turning it into a press.
Dropping too deep into shoulder discomfort.
Changing elbow bend during the rep.

reverse_pec_deck.description: A machine rear-delt fly that trains the rear shoulders and upper back with a stable path.
instructions: Face the pec deck machine and adjust the seat so the handles are at shoulder height.
Grip the handles with arms extended and chest supported against the pad.
Open the arms out and back by squeezing the rear delts and upper back.
Return under control without letting the weight stack slam.
formCues: Lead with elbows and rear delts.
Keep shoulders down away from the ears.
Use light enough weight to pause at the contracted position.
commonMistakes: Using traps instead of rear delts.
Going too heavy and losing the path.
Arching the back to finish reps.

wrist_curl.description: A direct forearm exercise that trains wrist flexion.
instructions: Sit with your forearms supported on your thighs or a bench, palms facing up.
Let the dumbbells roll slightly toward the fingers while keeping control.
Curl the wrists upward as far as comfortable and squeeze the forearms.
Lower slowly through the wrist without moving the elbows.
formCues: Move only at the wrist.
Use a light load and full control.
Keep forearms supported.
commonMistakes: Moving the whole arm instead of the wrist.
Using too much weight.
Rushing the bottom position.

reverse_wrist_curl.description: A direct forearm exercise that trains wrist extension and the top side of the forearms.
instructions: Sit with forearms supported and palms facing down while holding light dumbbells.
Keep the elbows and forearms fixed in place.
Lift the backs of the hands upward by extending the wrists.
Lower slowly through the wrist without swinging.
formCues: Move only at the wrist.
Use a light load and full control.
Keep forearms supported.
commonMistakes: Moving the whole arm instead of the wrist.
Using too much weight.
Rushing the bottom position.

shrugs.description: A barbell upper-trap exercise built around controlled shoulder elevation.
instructions: Stand tall holding a barbell in front of your thighs with arms straight.
Brace your core and keep the neck long.
Shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears and pause briefly.
Lower under control without rolling the shoulders forward or back.
formCues: Shrug straight up and down.
Keep arms long.
Pause at the top without rolling the shoulders.
commonMistakes: Rolling the shoulders.
Bending the elbows to lift the weight.
Craning the neck forward.

dumbbell_shrugs.description: A dumbbell shrug variation that trains the upper traps with each side loaded independently.
instructions: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Brace your trunk and let the arms hang long.
Elevate the shoulders straight up and squeeze the traps at the top.
Lower slowly to a full stretch without rolling the shoulders.
formCues: Shrug straight up and down.
Keep arms long.
Pause at the top without rolling the shoulders.
commonMistakes: Rolling the shoulders.
Bending the elbows to lift the weight.
Craning the neck forward.

ab_wheel_rollout.description: A demanding anti-extension core exercise using an ab wheel or barbell rollout.
instructions: Kneel with the ab wheel under your shoulders and hands on the handles.
Tuck the pelvis slightly and brace as if preparing for a plank.
Roll forward only as far as you can control without the lower back sagging.
Pull back to the start by driving the ribs down and using the abs, not the hips.
formCues: Brace as if resisting movement.
Keep ribs stacked over pelvis.
Breathe without losing tension.
commonMistakes: Holding breath the entire time.
Letting ribs flare or hips sag.
Choosing a version too advanced to control.

dead_bug.description: A low-impact core stability drill that trains rib, pelvis, and limb control.
instructions: Lie on your back with arms pointed up and hips and knees bent to 90 degrees.
Press your lower back gently toward the floor and brace your abs.
Extend one arm and the opposite leg while keeping the torso still.
Return to the start and alternate sides without arching the lower back.
formCues: Brace as if resisting movement.
Keep ribs stacked over pelvis.
Breathe without losing tension.
commonMistakes: Holding breath the entire time.
Letting ribs flare or hips sag.
Choosing a version too advanced to control.

bird_dog.description: A core and lower-back stability drill that trains opposite-arm and opposite-leg control.
instructions: Start on hands and knees with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Brace lightly and keep your spine neutral.
Reach one arm forward and the opposite leg back without rotating the hips.
Pause, then return under control and alternate sides.
formCues: Brace as if resisting movement.
Keep ribs stacked over pelvis.
Breathe without losing tension.
commonMistakes: Holding breath the entire time.
Letting ribs flare or hips sag.
Choosing a version too advanced to control.

side_plank.description: A side-body core hold that trains the obliques to resist lateral bending.
instructions: Lie on your side with elbow under shoulder and legs stacked or staggered.
Lift your hips so your body forms a straight line from head to feet.
Brace the abs and squeeze the glutes while breathing steadily.
Hold until you can no longer keep the hips lifted and torso aligned.
formCues: Brace as if resisting movement.
Keep ribs stacked over pelvis.
Breathe without losing tension.
commonMistakes: Holding breath the entire time.
Letting ribs flare or hips sag.
Choosing a version too advanced to control.

mountain_climber.description: A bodyweight conditioning drill that combines plank stability with fast knee drives.
instructions: Start in a high plank with hands under shoulders and body in a straight line.
Brace your abs and keep shoulders stacked over hands.
Drive one knee toward the chest, then quickly switch legs.
Move at a pace that lets you keep hips controlled and ribs down.
formCues: Set a pace you can sustain with clean form.
Keep breathing rhythmic.
Maintain posture as fatigue builds.
commonMistakes: Letting speed destroy technique.
Holding the breath during hard efforts.
Starting too fast and fading early.

battle_ropes.description: A conditioning exercise using heavy ropes to train shoulders, arms, core, and work capacity.
instructions: Stand in an athletic stance holding one rope end in each hand.
Brace your trunk and keep knees softly bent.
Create powerful alternating or simultaneous waves by driving the arms from the shoulders.
Maintain posture and rhythm for the target time without letting the lower back collapse.
formCues: Set a pace you can sustain with clean form.
Keep breathing rhythmic.
Maintain posture as fatigue builds.
commonMistakes: Letting speed destroy technique.
Holding the breath during hard efforts.
Starting too fast and fading early.

rower.description: A rowing-machine cardio movement that uses the legs, hips, back, and arms in sequence.
instructions: Sit on the rower with feet strapped in and hands holding the handle.
Start at the catch with knees bent, torso slightly forward, and arms long.
Drive with the legs first, then swing the torso back and pull the handle to the lower ribs.
Recover by extending arms, hinging forward, and bending knees back to the catch.
formCues: Think legs, body, arms on the drive.
Recover arms, body, legs on the way forward.
Keep strokes smooth rather than frantic.
commonMistakes: Pulling with the arms before using the legs.
Rushing the slide on the recovery.
Rounding the lower back at the catch.

assault_bike.description: A fan-bike conditioning movement using both arms and legs for high-output intervals.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your knee stays slightly bent at the bottom of the pedal stroke.
Grip the handles, sit tall, and begin pedaling smoothly.
Push and pull the handles in rhythm with your legs.
Keep your cadence and breathing controlled for the target interval.
formCues: Push and pull the handles with the arms.
Keep your torso quiet on the seat.
Build cadence gradually before sprinting.
commonMistakes: Death-gripping the handles and tensing the neck.
Bouncing side to side on the seat.
Starting every interval too hard.

sled_push.description: A loaded conditioning and leg-drive movement that trains quads, glutes, calves, and bracing.
instructions: Load the sled appropriately and grip the handles with arms straight or slightly bent.
Lean your body forward into the sled while keeping a strong line from head to heel.
Drive the floor away with powerful steps and steady pressure through the hands.
Keep moving for the target distance without letting your hips rise too high.
formCues: Keep constant pressure into the handles.
Drive knees forward with strong foot contact.
Match body angle to the load.
commonMistakes: Letting hips rise so the legs stop driving.
Taking long, slow steps that kill momentum.
Rounding the back under fatigue.

medicine_ball_slam.description: An explosive conditioning movement using the abs, lats, and shoulders to slam a medicine ball.
instructions: Stand with feet about shoulder width and hold the medicine ball at chest height.
Raise the ball overhead while bracing your trunk.
Slam the ball forcefully into the floor by using your abs, lats, and hips.
Catch or pick up the ball safely, reset your posture, and repeat.
formCues: Move through the trunk, not just the arms.
Keep the motion controlled.
Stop before the lower back takes over.
commonMistakes: Using momentum instead of abdominal control.
Pulling on the neck or shoulders.
Arching the lower back between reps.

barbell_incline_press.description: An upper-chest barbell press that allows heavier loading than dumbbell incline variations.
instructions: Set the bench to a low-to-moderate incline and position your eyes under the bar.
Grip slightly wider than shoulder width and set your shoulder blades into the bench.
Unrack the bar and lower it toward the upper chest with control.
Press up and slightly back until the elbows are straight without flaring excessively.
formCues: Use a low-to-moderate incline rather than turning it into a shoulder press.
Keep ribs down and shoulder blades stable.
Press up and slightly back in a natural arc.
commonMistakes: Setting the bench too steep.
Overarching the lower back.
Letting the weights drift too far forward.

cable_row.description: A cable row variation for training the lats and mid-back with constant tension.
instructions: Set the cable at about mid-torso height and hold the chosen handle with arms extended.
Brace your trunk and keep the shoulders down.
Pull the handle toward your ribs by driving elbows back.
Return slowly until the arms are long without losing posture.
formCues: Row elbows toward the hips or lower ribs.
Keep the torso stable throughout the rep.
Squeeze the back without yanking with the arms.
commonMistakes: Jerking the weight with momentum.
Rounding the back or craning the neck.
Shrugging instead of rowing.

single_leg_rdl.description: A unilateral hinge that trains hamstrings, glutes, balance, and hip stability.
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell or kettlebell in one or both hands.
Slightly bend the working knee and hinge by sending the free leg back.
Lower until you feel a hamstring stretch while keeping hips mostly square.
Drive through the working foot to stand tall without twisting.
formCues: Push hips back before bending more at the knees.
Keep the load close to your body.
Stand by driving hips through, not by leaning back.
commonMistakes: Rounding the lower back.
Letting the load drift away from the body.
Squatting the movement instead of hinging.

seated_dumbbell_press.description: A seated dumbbell overhead press that supports the torso while training shoulder strength.
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support and bring the dumbbells to shoulder height.
Plant your feet and keep your ribs down against the bench.
Press the dumbbells overhead until arms are straight and shoulders stay controlled.
Lower to shoulder height under control before the next rep.
formCues: Brace ribs down before pressing.
Finish with biceps near ears.
Press without shrugging into your neck.
commonMistakes: Leaning back excessively.
Pressing with wrists bent back.
Shrugging before the arms finish.

ez_bar_curl.description: A biceps curl using an angled EZ bar that can be more comfortable for wrists and elbows.
instructions: Stand tall holding the EZ bar with an underhand grip on the angled sections.
Keep elbows close to your torso and shoulders relaxed.
Curl the bar upward until the biceps are fully contracted.
Lower slowly to straight arms without swinging.
formCues: Keep elbows close to the torso.
Use a full squeeze at the top.
Lower slowly to straight arms.
commonMistakes: Swinging the torso.
Letting elbows drift far forward.
Only performing the top half of the rep.

rope_pushdown.description: A rope cable pushdown that trains the triceps and allows the hands to separate at lockout.
instructions: Attach a rope to a high cable and hold one end in each hand.
Pin your elbows by your sides and keep your chest tall.
Push the rope down and separate the ends slightly at the bottom.
Return slowly until the forearms rise while the elbows stay fixed.
formCues: Keep upper arms as still as possible.
Reach full elbow extension at the finish.
Control the stretch without letting shoulders take over.
commonMistakes: Letting elbows flare or drift.
Turning the movement into a press.
Rushing the stretched position.
